title: Ultra Crispy Japanese Chicken Skewers
description: Fatty and velvety, these Chugoku-inspired chicken skewers are a show-off dish featuring sunflower oil and a satvik-friendly flavor profile.
instructions: Cut the chicken breast into 1-inch cubes and thread them onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sugar, and 2 tablespoons of sunflower oil to make a marinade.
Add the chicken skewers to the marinade and mix well to coat, then let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Heat 2 tablespoons of sunflower oil in a large grill pan over medium-high heat.
Remove the chicken skewers from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Grill the chicken skewers for 5-7 minutes per side, or until they are nicely charred and cooked through.
Meanwhile, mix together green onions, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
Once the chicken skewers are done, brush them with yuzu juice and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Transfer the chicken skewers to a serving platter and garnish with green onion mixture.
